text: Source URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/180115 Source Type: Rate My Professors Document Topic: Professor teaching style, lab instruction, workload, participation, and student experience School: CSU East Bay Department: Physics / Astronomy Course: PHYS125, PHYS138, ASTR139 Content: Review 1: A student described this professor as one of the best lab instructors they had for PHYS125. The student said he may seem harsh when students are disrespectful or not paying attention, but he ap

he student said he may seem harsh when students are disrespectful or not paying attention, but he appreciates participation and effort. The student also said the lab lectures are clear, lab reports are manageable, students get three days to submit them, and grading is generally lenient with only minor deductions. Review 2: A student said this professor was funny, caring, and helpful in PHYS125 lecture and lab. The student mentioned that he helps with XL and labs, drops the lowest lab grade, and

ture and lab. The student mentioned that he helps with XL and labs, drops the lowest lab grade, and is approachable during office hours. The student also said he does not allow lab makeups, but the labs are manageable within the lab period, and exams are fairly easy. Review 3: A student described ASTR139 as a synchronous online class with one long weekly class session. The student said the class can be manageable for an A if students are willing to sit through the weekly session. The course incl

an be manageable for an A if students are willing to sit through the weekly session. The course includes breakout rooms, group labs, and participation-based work, but the class can feel long and frustrating. Review 4: A student said ASTR139 included weekly lab assignments that were not difficult but felt like busy work. The student said attendance was not mandatory, but students often needed help with the lab assignments. The student also described the professor as condescending and passive aggr

with the lab assignments. The student also described the professor as condescending and passive aggressive when students asked for help. Review 5: A student described PHYS138 negatively and said the professor could be condescending, rude, and humiliating when students answered questions incorrectly. The student said the professor sometimes asked questions ahead of the current lecture material and explained concepts in a way that felt insulting. Notes: This document is useful for questions about

explained concepts in a way that felt insulting. Notes: This document is useful for questions about lab instruction, lecture clarity, workload, grading leniency, participation, office hours, group labs, online class structure, and mixed student experiences. Since this source is for Physics and Astronomy courses, it should be used as general professor-quality context rather than as a main Computer Science professor review.

se requires a lot of personal effort and can feel like two classes worth of work. ading, homework, quizzes, learning value, and whether students recommend taking Professor Christopher Smith during a busy semester. Students describe the work as time consuming but useful.

The student also said the key to passing is completing the assigned problems and asking questions during office hours. ching additional YouTube videos, practicing assigned problems repeatedly, and going to office hours. lability, and support. The student said he focuses on lectures, mechanisms, extra credit, and curves. ing class, asking questions, and correcting him when needed because he may offer extra credit. The student said they still worked hard, but his explanations made the second course easier. They said the course is tough and curve-heavy, but he teaches clearly, shows students how to solve problems, and tells them what they need to know for quizzes and tests. The student said students who attend lectures, practic

what they need to know for quizzes and tests. The student said students who attend lectures, practice, and put in effort are more likely to succeed. ially for students who want an affordable, accredited school in the Bay Area. about CSU East Bay. rs and some weaker ones. Overall, the Quora discussion presents CSU East Bay as an affordable, safe, accredited, and underrated university with some strong academic programs. text: Source URL: https://www.reddit.com/r/CSUEB/comments/1q4iw46/how_are_the_quality_of_the_professors_and_classes/ Source Type: Reddit thread Document Topic: General student opinions about professor and class quality at CSU East Bay School: CSU East Bay Department: General / STEM context Content: Original post: I've heard some mixed reviews and the negative saying some don't know what they're doing or that the school is a joke. Since East Bay isn't some renowned university I wouldn't expect the qual

that the school is a joke. Since East Bay isn't some renowned university I wouldn't expect the quality to be super high but I wouldn't expect it to be bad. Any insight would be great. Comment 1: A professor at CSUEB explains that CSU campuses are teaching universities, meaning faculty generally teach a full course load and are focused on teaching first. They explain that research universities often focus more on research, publishing, grants, and graduate education, while CSU students may get mo

en focus more on research, publishing, grants, and graduate education, while CSU students may get more direct instruction from faculty. Comment 2: A student who earned a master's at CSUEB said they enjoyed the small class sizes, faculty, and student population. They said professors came from industry, wanted to teach at a smaller school, and brought real-world knowledge into the classroom. Comment 3: A math major said their instructors have been amazing and compared them positively to instructor

3: A math major said their instructors have been amazing and compared them positively to instructors they had at SJSU and UCSC. They said many instructors are knowledgeable about current learning methods and also do original research. Comment 4: A student who primarily took STEM classes said the professors they had were fantastic. Comment 5: A graduate said they had a great experience, most professors were engaged, and the instruction was quality. They said one or two professors were only okay,

ssors were engaged, and the instruction was quality. They said one or two professors were only okay, but overall they felt prepared for graduate school. Comment 6: Another commenter said the experience can be hit or miss, with mostly good professors and a few bad ones, but overall it was a good experience. Comment 7: A commenter said students get more face time with professors at CSU East Bay compared to a research university, where students may spend more time with teaching assistants. Notes: T

ared to a research university, where students may spend more time with teaching assistants. Notes: This document is useful for questions about overall professor quality, teaching focus, class size, faculty engagement, STEM classes, and the difference between CSU East Bay and larger research universities.
